claims.evidence_text: STB, SNB, FHB, KB, and LS are important wheat diseases, worldwide. Each of these diseases can cause 10–15% yield losses, which can sometimes approach 50% in favourable weather conditions, especially in low-input agriculture, where disease control is generally poor (Duveiller et al. 2007).
claims.claim_summary: Each of the major wheat diseases (septoria tritici blotch, septoria nodorum blotch, fusarium head blight, karnal bunt, leaf spot) can cause 10-15% yield loss, up to ~50% in favourable weather.

claims.evidence_text: Only a solitary MQTL (MQTL2B.2) provided resistance to all the 5 diseases; each of ten MQTLs (1A.1, 1B.4, 2A.1, 2B.2, 2D.3, 3B.1, 3B.2, 3B.3, 4A.1, 4B.3, 5B.3) provided resistance to 3–4 diseases
claims.claim_summary: In wheat, MQTL2B.2 confers multiple disease resistance to all five diseases (STB, SNB, FHB, KB, LS); ten further MQTLs confer resistance to 3-4 diseases each.

claims.evidence_text: MQTL1B.1 is co-localized with two genes viz., Chs-1B and Stb11, which provide resistance against KB and STB, respectively.
claims.claim_summary: Wheat MQTL1B.1 co-locates with resistance genes Chs-1B (karnal bunt) and Stb11 (septoria tritici blotch), a hotspot for SNB, STB and KB resistance.

claims.evidence_text: the wheat Sr2 locus, which provides resistance to stem rust, leaf rust, and powdery mildew (Mago et al. 2011), was tightly linked in the repulsion phase to the Fhb1 locus, which confers resistance to FHB (Flemmig, 2012).
claims.claim_summary: Wheat Sr2 locus confers resistance to stem rust, leaf rust and powdery mildew; Fhb1 locus confers resistance to fusarium head blight (FHB).
